response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web-focused engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is a poor fit for this role on multiple hard-requirement dimensions. Most critically, the role requires native-level Japanese language proficiency — Bogdan lists English, Romanian, and basic German only. Beyond the language blocker, the role demands deep embedded C/Linux-kernel expertise for LAN-switch security features (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC authentication, L2/L3 switching), and Bogdan's background is overwhelmingly web, TypeScript/Node.js, and application-layer software. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with no evidence of Linux kernel modification or embedded networking security work.
strengths: Extensive overall software engineering experience (15+ years) spanning web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Demonstrated low-latency and systems-level thinking (async I/O, high-performance services at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — indicating disciplined engineering habits
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for the role
No relevant security domain experience: zero evidence of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption protocols, or device attack/tamper detection
C and Linux kernel skills are superficial; the role demands deep embedded C development and kernel source modification capability, which are absent from his profile
